title: Percent, Rates, Markups, Discounts, and Repeated Change · Guided Modeling Studio
summary: Audit prices, discounts, taxes, tips, population changes, and performance claims. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a consumer percent-claim audit while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify the comparison base before calculating a percent, percentage, or whole.
Calculate absolute and relative change and explain when each comparison is useful.
Model markup, discount, tax, and tip as ordered multiplicative factors.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-beginner cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.why: The Percent Claims result for a consumer percent-claim audit is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.
